questions.question: What's the actual impact of fees and slippage on retail-size paper-traded crypto strategies?
questions.summary: ~$1/round-trip on $200 positions (0.25% taker both legs). Gross +$1,085 became net -$782 on the 3-month backtest — the delta is the answer. Fees erase thin edges.

questions.question: Which parameter ranges of Bollinger and MA-crossover are robust to regime shifts, and which are overfit?
questions.summary: None are robust: all 12 variants with n>100 negative over 6 months net of costs. Per-regime conditioning untested (rung 3).

questions.question: (Emergent) Do constraints or strategies determine outcomes at this scale?
questions.summary: Constraints dominate: 99.6% of candidate signals died on cooldown/exposure/concurrency. Candidate fitness = edge per constraint slot, not signal frequency.

questions.question: Does sentiment data (Fear & Greed) improve technical strategies, or is it noise?
questions.summary: Layer 2 (context) never went live; deferred past Phase 1b per one-factor-at-a-time.

questions.question: Does the walk-forward tuner produce variants that hold up out-of-sample?
questions.summary: tune.py never built; compare.py (the scoring instrument) now exists. Blocked on the candidate roster accumulating live trades.

learnings.claim: GitHub Actions free-tier */5 cron delivers only ~3-5% of scheduled invocations (~24/day vs 288 assumed), not the documented cadence.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-23 migration entry (69/1712 = 4.0% over six days) and 2026-05-18 entry (~60 min between runs)

learnings.claim: Migrating cron to a Hetzner CX22 VPS clears the 95% uptime gate mechanically without amending Phase 2 entry criteria.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 47-day-gap entry: 11,574 runs all status='ok', gate 1 of 3 now mechanically satisfied

learnings.claim: The 95% uptime architectural gate (Phase 2 gate 1 of 3) is now satisfied.
Both base strategies (Bollinger and MA-crossover) lose money over a real 6-month window and are retired from the active roster.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 retirement entry: net -$1,719.44 over 6 months, bollinger_default n=539 -$636.75

learnings.claim: The shared-position-ceiling architecture works as specified: adding variants changes P&L only via selection within the cap, not via added exposure.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 front-loaded-12-variants entry (99.6% rejection, exposure never breached)

learnings.claim: The fee-floor finding replicated on novel LLM-surfaced strategies: gross-positive edges are smaller than the ~0.6% round-trip cost.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 gauntlet entry: all five candidates net-negative; weekend_illiquidity_momentum gross-positive (Sharpe 1.51) but fee-negative

learnings.claim: No LLM-surfaced candidate earned promotion; all five gauntlet candidates lost money net of fees.

Cost engineering via maker fills is a validated, mechanically reliable lever: maker beat taker in 12/12 matched pairs, ~$0.21-0.24 fees saved per trade, with near-zero fill loss because the strategy buys into falling price.
The first net-positive configuration (maker/72h/tp8%/sl4%, +$0.031/slot) has a statistically zero edge — inside noise and subject to multiple-comparisons bias, so it is not run against the holdout.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 first net-positive cell entry: +$0.03/trade over 146 trades, best of 24 cells

learnings.claim: Nearly all of the project's learning was produced in the ~48 hours of 2026-07-16/17; the ~80 days of elapsed calendar time did not contribute the learning it was narrativized as producing.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 correction entry ('the sequence was valuable; the duration was not')

learnings.claim: The one incompressible resource (live trade-days for the A/B) was not accumulating during the elapsed weeks; the live clock started 2026-07-16, so eleven calendar weeks bought one day of the slow currency.
The claude.ai Gmail connector is draft-only and cannot send email, forcing digest delivery to VPS-side SMTP.
Hetzner blocks outbound port 465 but leaves 587 open, so digest email must use SMTP via 587/STARTTLS.
Growing LENSES from 5 to 8 silently pushed synthesis output past max_tokens, producing a truncated tool input that validated as {} and died with a misleading 'field missing' error.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 seam-closed entry (bug found and hardened against)

learnings.claim: The autonomous pipeline now detects failures within ~24h rather than preventing them; total silence (no digest email) is the last-resort alarm.

No real credential was ever committed to the repo; every .env.template version held only placeholders and .env was never tracked — the May 2026 exposure surface was the session transcript, not the repository.
Deferring incident logging is costly: the May 2026 .env exposure record was written ~60 days late from memory, and the details that would matter in a real postmortem are gone.

The infrastructure ran clean for 47 operator-absent days (11,490+ fetch commits, zero gaps over ~1h) while signals/trades/decisions/llm_calls stayed at 0 and curriculum artifacts went unedited — confirming the W21 bear case that 'the infrastructure was the project.'
Ten ideas died in the same corner of design space (short-horizon taker-fee mean-reversion), so searching a structurally different space beats sampling the old space harder.
Epitaphs/verdicts in dead-ideas.json are conditional on the cost/horizon model that produced them, so a new lever partially invalidates those verdicts and requires re-gauntleting near-misses.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'the levers are the strategy' entry (horizon re-test of weekend, omori, vol_thrust, regime_gate, dead_zone)

learnings.claim: Deterministic hash-based signal rolls (plus the signals table UNIQUE constraint) make the live loop idempotent, avoiding the double-signaling and non-reproducibility that random() would cause.
Without Layer 4 exits the 5-position cap deadlocks the loop after 5 trades, which would have made the placebo prove the loop broken rather than working.
Replay's pnl_pct (which Sharpe uses) is gross of fees while pnl_usd is net, so Sharpe-on-gross must not be read as risk-adjusted net performance.
At ~10 trades/day per variant (~300 points/month), a factorial design lacks the sample size to populate even a 2x2x2 matrix, so one-factor-at-a-time produces the most learnable results at this scale.
learnings.evidence: 2026-04-26 'One factor at a time, not factorial' entry

hypotheses.commitment: By end of the first calendar week of cron operation, the runs table should contain ~2,016 rows with status='ok' (90%+ success rate); if below 90%, investigate before Week 2 roster review begins.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-17 — Curriculum anchor is first cron run
hypotheses.resolution: GitHub Actions delivered only ~4% invocation rate (69/1712 over six days); the platform ceiling made the 90% target unachievable, superseded by the 2026-05-23 amendment splitting invocation and code-success metrics.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the operator commits a decision-log entry choosing scheduler option (a), (b), or (c); if no decision is committed by end of Week 2, the scheduler issue becomes the project's most expensive un-made call and the Week 3 build doesn't begin until it's made.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-18 — GH cron cadence does not match assumed 5-min
hypotheses.resolution: The 2026-05-23 entry migrated the cron to a Hetzner VPS, resolving the scheduler question.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the Claude-mediated synthesis will produce either (a) a defensible justification for keeping Bollinger and MA-crossover naming which modern-filter criteria they pass/fail, or (b) a defensible alternative roster with the same justification structure; if neither, Week 3 starts a postmortem on the reframe itself.
hypotheses.resolution: The 2026-07-02 retirement entry retired both base strategies on 6-month evidence and deferred replacements to LLM-surfaced candidates; the reframe's canon-first sequence was later affirmed as having earned its keep (2026-07-17 correction).

hypotheses.commitment: Amended reliability hypothesis: invocation rate (VPS-controlled, target >=99%) and code success rate (project-controlled, target >=95%) tracked separately; combined target 95%+ uptime of expected runs over any 4-week window.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-23 — Migrating cron from GitHub Actions to Hetzner VPS
hypotheses.resolution: 2026-07-02 entry records 11,574 runs all status='ok' over the 47-day window; the 95% uptime gate is mechanically satisfied.

hypotheses.commitment: rejection_streak_gated_ignition / the gate family gets an out-of-sample re-test on the NEXT 180d window before any gate-family idea is judged again; that result decides whether the gate fits the current regime or best-of-round is what recency overfit looks like.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-17 — Seam closed for real; first fully autonomous cycle ran

hypotheses.commitment: If null_baseline shows a positive edge over a real sample, something is wrong with the simulator, fee model, or market data — investigate before believing any candidate's results.

Registered top-2 arms (weekend_illiquidity_momentum, volume_thrust_regime_shift) are experiment arms, not promotions; expected outcome is all three arms lose slowly and candidates must prove they lose less than random, promotion bar unchanged (beat null at p<0.05 over 100+ closed trades).
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-07-16 — Gauntlet results: top 2 registered as A/B arms
